title: NDA 2026 Preparation Guide: Complete Syllabus, Study Plan, Books & Expert Strategy
author: DefenceRoger Editorial
data: The National Defence Academy (NDA) Exam 2026, conducted by the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC), is the gateway for candidates aspiring to join the Indian Army, Indian Navy, and Indian Air Force after Class 12. Every year, lakhs of students compete for a limited number of vacancies, making the examination highly competitive. Success requires a combination of conceptual clarity, disciplined preparation, and a smart study strategy. The NDA written examination consists of two papers: Mathematics (300 marks) and the General Ability Test (GAT) carrying 600 marks, making a total of 900 marks. Mathematics includes important topics such as Algebra, Matrices and Determinants, Trigonometry, Analytical Geometry, Differential Calculus, Integral Calculus, Vector Algebra, Statistics, and Probability. Aspirants should begin with NCERT Class 11 and 12 books before moving to advanced reference material and should regularly revise important formulas to improve speed and accuracy. The General Ability Test comprises English, Physics, Chemistry, General Science, History, Geography, and Current Affairs. Candidates should focus on strengthening grammar, vocabulary, comprehension skills, scientific concepts, Indian history, geography, and stay updated with national and international current affairs by reading newspapers daily. A well-planned study schedule is essential for effective preparation. Allocate dedicated time for Mathematics and GAT every day, revise previously studied topics regularly, and maintain concise notes for quick revision. Practicing mock tests and solving at least ten years of NDA Previous Year Question Papers (PYQs) is one of the most effective ways to understand the exam pattern, improve time management, identify weak areas, and build confidence. Aspirants preparing without coaching can also crack the examination by following the official syllabus, limiting resources to standard books, maintaining consistency, and revising regularly. Common mistakes such as ignoring the syllabus, studying from too many books, skipping revision, or neglecting current affairs should be avoided. With disciplined preparation, consistent practice, and the right strategy, candidates can significantly improve their chances of clearing the NDA written examination and moving one step closer to achieving their dream of serving the nation as officers in the Indian Armed Forces.
